name: Sarah
manager_notes: Sarah is delivering, but there are signs she may be over-rotating on execution while underweighting influence and visibility. I want to understand whether she has a clear view of the performance bar at Senior level and where she sees gaps in her current approach. This conversation should test how she frames impact, how she handles cross-functional friction, and whether she has a practical plan to raise signal with stakeholders instead of waiting for good work to be noticed automatically.
answers: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.
That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I want to lead one cross-team decision this cycle, end to end.
Specific feedback checkpoints during projects would help me level up faster.
I will choose one recurring deliverable and define what good looks like before the next review cycle.
